why_it_matters: The severe overrun outcome could pull attention downstream, but the runway-entry candidate point is well anchored before acceleration.
mitigation: Keep review anchored at wrong-runway entry and position-verification moment.

why_it_matters: ATC and airport-construction context can displace cockpit verification duties if left uncontrolled.
mitigation: Explicitly retain captain taxi lane and first officer monitoring lane as primary actor frame.

why_it_matters: First officer recall is unavailable, so the final missed-cue sequence is partly reconstructed.
mitigation: State the reconstruction limit and avoid overclaiming the exact last missed cue.

why_it_matters: Late low-energy awareness and impact severity can tempt review toward the final seconds rather than the earlier automation-state boundary.
mitigation: Keep candidate review at the FLCH/A-P disconnect/idle thrust/A-T HOLD transition and treat later awareness as downstream.

why_it_matters: ATC speed restriction and autoflight logic can displace cockpit monitoring if not tightly bounded.
mitigation: Keep direct actor framing inside the cockpit and treat ATC/system as context only.

why_it_matters: Automation-state interpretation is central and can overdominate the human monitoring lane.
mitigation: Use explicit author note that technical logic matters but does not replace crew monitoring and control review.

why_it_matters: Tree strike and CFIT outcome may pull focus to alerts and final seconds instead of the earlier descent-continuation boundary.
mitigation: Anchor review before EGPWS and emphasize the MDA-continuation segment.

why_it_matters: Dispatch and ATC context can pull the review away from the cockpit descent-management lane.
mitigation: Retain captain PF and first officer PM duties as the primary actor frame.

why_it_matters: FMC setup and procedure framing can overshadow the direct continuation decision through minimums.
mitigation: Separate setup context from the later V/S and MDA continuation action lane.
